title: Cloud serverless console backend (AWS Lambda + EFS + IoT Core)
description:
## Intent
Deliver a multi-tenant AWS cloud runtime for `kbsc` using Lambda + EFS + API Gateway + IoT Core.

## Scope
- Read-only v1 backend
- GitHub webhook-driven EFS sync
- Cognito auth with strict tenant isolation
- MQTT-over-WSS as primary realtime channel
- SSE as compatibility fallback

## Definition of Done
- Python CDK stack deploys with `AWS_PROFILE=anthus`.
- Lambda serves console APIs from EFS tenant roots.
- IoT topic model `projects/{account}/{project}/events` is enforced by policy.
- GitHub webhook queue/worker sync publishes realtime events.
- Console frontend consumes MQTT-over-WSS with SSE fallback.
- Runbook + alarms + E2E launch checklist are complete.
creator: ryan.porter

Next: continue with auth/isolation implementation (5.3) while 5.1 waits for Docker-unblocked deploy run.

comments.text:

Next: resolve stack cleanup lock and re-establish IaC-only deployment flow before closing 5.4/5.7.
Risk/Blocker: temporary stack drift and UPDATE_COMPLETE_CLEANUP_IN_PROGRESS still active.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:

Next: complete final realtime/browser validation and close 5.4/5.7 when evidence is captured.

Next: unblock webhook secret permissions, complete signed ingress + browser MQTT validation, then close 5.4/5.7.

comments.author: ryan.porter

Evidence: latest smokes show unauth /dev returns 401 and auth-protected tenant endpoints return 200 once token is provided.
Next: implement Hosted UI PKCE, auth bootstrap endpoint, API token registry + IoT custom authorizer, and tenant-claim enforcement changes.
Risk/Blocker: signed webhook E2E still blocked by Secrets Manager permission gap.
